key_questions: Can this company compound free cash flow with durable moats and disciplined capital returns?
What would falsify the thesis quickly (measurable triggers)?
What is the bear case and what evidence would support it?

sources.title: SEC XBRL time-series (AAPL)
excerpt_or_key_points: Fixture: time-series extract representing SEC XBRL submissions (for offline demo).

blocks.text: Anchor KPI baseline: reported metric is {{N1}}.
Base case: the edge is durable only if reported KPIs continue to support the core drivers; size only after evidence coverage is broad.
Assumption: we will ingest primary filings and transcripts to expand Tier one and Tier three coverage before taking a meaningful position.
This report is constrained to collected evidence; claims not supported by sources are marked as assumptions or omitted.
Variant perception is the delta between what consensus must be underwriting and what our evidence can support today.
Assumption: incremental sources will reduce contradictions and make scenario work numerically stable.
There exists a sourced KPI baseline ({{N1}}) in the evidence library. Falsifier: the baseline cannot be reproduced from Tier one sources.
The underwriting is that durable value drivers are visible in reported KPIs before they are visible in narrative. Falsifier: narrative claims persist without KPI confirmation across multiple periods.
Assumption: capture mechanisms remain durable through the cycle. Falsifier: sustained evidence of pricing pressure or commoditization from independent sources.
Primary source coverage is captured in the evidence library, including publisher, timestamps, and raw text references when available.
Decision-relevant overview should be limited to drivers we can verify and monitor, not generic company description.
Assumption: reported KPIs map to underwriting drivers without definition drift; reconcile definitions when full filings are ingested.
Competitor primary sources are not collected in this demo run; moat claims are intentionally limited.
Moat assessment should separate durable switching costs and ecosystem gravity from transient product cycles.
Assumption: lock-in is durable; validate with churn proxies, attach trends, and competitive win-loss evidence.
Numeric facts are structured with value, unit, period, source, and accessed timestamp to enable auditability.
Financial quality should be judged on cash conversion, margin durability, and dilution discipline, not single-quarter beats.
Assumption: KPI coverage is sufficient to compute margins and cash conversion; if not, add the missing concepts before inference.
This demo run does not ingest market price data; valuation numbers are intentionally omitted until price tape is collected.
Scenario work should make drivers explicit and quantify what must be true for the current price to be rational.
Assumption: discount rate and terminal assumptions are within a reasonable band; verify via market data ingestion.
No dated catalyst sources are included in this run; catalysts are treated as unknown until sourced.
Catalysts should be framed as information releases that change scenario weights, not as calendar events alone.
Assumption: disclosure cadence will be consistent; validate via transcript ingestion and event tracking.
The risk officer pass enumerates disconfirming evidence to seek and falsifiers tied to measurable triggers.
Pre-mortem: We shipped a compelling story without sufficient Tier one and Tier two coverage, and the thesis failed on basic accounting or KPI reality.
Assumption: additional Tier one and Tier three sources will materially improve decision quality; proceed only after evidence gaps close.
Change-mind triggers must be measurable and tied to collected sources; otherwise they are not monitorable.
We change our mind when disconfirming evidence persists across multiple independent sources and cannot be explained by timing.
Assumption: monitoring will include filings, transcripts, and price tape; until then, posture should remain conservative.

exhibits.title: KPI Table (KPI)
exhibits.question: What are the anchor KPIs and what is the reported baseline?
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Establish a sourced KPI baseline before layering interpretation.

exhibits.title: Margins (Margin)
exhibits.question: What is the operating leverage profile over time?
data_summary: N/A in this run (margin series not yet collected).
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Do not assert margin trends without sourced series.

exhibits.title: FCF (FCF)
exhibits.question: Is free cash flow conversion durable across cycles?
data_summary: N/A in this run (free cash flow series not yet collected).
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Cash conversion is the decision metric; collect it before sizing risk.

exhibits.title: SBC and Dilution (SBC / dilution)
exhibits.question: Is dilution controlled relative to cash generation?
data_summary: N/A in this run (share count and SBC series not yet collected).
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Treat dilution as a recurring expense unless proven otherwise.

exhibits.title: Scenario Drivers (Scenario driver)
exhibits.question: Which variables drive scenario outcomes?
data_summary: Key drivers to quantify: mix, margins, cash conversion, and capital returns (sources pending).
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Explicit drivers prevent narrative drift.

exhibits.title: Sensitivity (Sensitivity)
exhibits.question: How sensitive is the thesis to a small number of variables?
data_summary: Sensitivity is N/A until valuation inputs and price tape are collected.
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Sensitivity is how you separate conviction from leverage.

evidence_table.claim: Anchor KPI baseline exists ({{N1}}) and is reproducible from Tier one sources.

whats_missing: Primary filing text for narrative disclosures (risk factors, segment mix).
Price tape and valuation inputs to quantify scenarios and sensitivity.
Transcript sources to validate management commentary versus filings.
